records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.1, does your Plan for Software Aspects of Certification describe:
records.query: An overview of the system and how software fits into it — functions, architecture, and the hardware/software split?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.1, does your Plan for Software Aspects of Certification describe:
records.query: An overview of the software itself, including each software component and its assigned software level?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.1, does your Plan for Software Aspects of Certification describe:
records.query: The certification basis, and how you determined the software level(s)?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.1, does your Plan for Software Aspects of Certification describe:
records.query: The software life cycle you will follow — the processes from planning through delivery?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.1, does your Plan for Software Aspects of Certification describe:
records.query: Every life-cycle document (data item) you plan to produce, and which ones the FAA will see?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.1, does your Plan for Software Aspects of Certification describe:
records.query: Your schedule for the major software milestones?

records.section: Section 2 — Anything Unusual About Your Project
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.1 (additional considerations), does the PSAC call out:
records.query: Reused or previously developed software, COTS software, or option-selectable software?

records.section: Section 2 — Anything Unusual About Your Project
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.1 (additional considerations), does the PSAC call out:
records.query: Any tools you expect to qualify — and why they need qualification?

records.section: Section 2 — Anything Unusual About Your Project
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.1 (additional considerations), does the PSAC call out:
records.query: Any alternative methods of compliance you plan to propose?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178C §11.1 and FAA supplier-oversight expectations:
records.query: If suppliers develop any of your software, does the PSAC explain how you will oversee them?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.20, does your Software Accomplishment Summary:
records.query: Mirror the PSAC's overviews, and state clearly where the project deviated from the plan?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.20, does your Software Accomplishment Summary:
records.query: Identify exactly what software was built — part numbers, versions, and key characteristics?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.20, does your Software Accomplishment Summary:
Based on DO-178B/C §11.20, does your Software Accomplishment Summary:
records.query: State the software's status — including every open problem report and its effect on safety?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.20, does your Software Accomplishment Summary:
records.query: Include a compliance statement: your declaration that the software satisfies its objectives?
For modified software, did you analyze the impact of the changes (a change impact analysis) and keep the results?

records.subsection: Cross-cutting sanity checks before you submit:
records.query: Do the PSAC and SAS agree with each other — same components, same software levels, same processes?

records.subsection: Cross-cutting sanity checks before you submit:
records.query: Did you do everything the PSAC promised — and where you didn't, does the SAS explain the deviation?
notes: This free checklist is a high-level sanity check — free to use and share. It doesn't include the item-level actions needed to fully close each objective; those live in AeroCert's comprehensive checklist, kept current as the standards change and available through donations. We'd love to hear how it worked for you — tell us at support@aerocert.org.
